human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1471 requirements for digital-publishing-specific execution, incident, access, change-readiness, metadata, content, approval, confidentiality, legal, editorial, accessibility, security, engineering, rights, contractual, and completion-evidence boundaries.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and professional-and-information-services sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes normal publication progression, missing metadata, authority-boundary refusal, scholarly content workflow, completion evidence refusal, system dependency failure, change-readiness gap, confidentiality conflict, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence escalation, conflicting policies, and tenant adaptation.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Outputs are bounded to evidence-backed digital publishing execution status, completion evidence, exception notes, system incident summaries, change-readiness escalations, tenant-adaptation summaries, and adjacent-owner routing rather than editorial, legal, rights, accessibility-certification, security, engineering, financial, contractual, or final signoff authority.

Source set includes W3C EPUB, EPUB Accessibility, and WCAG; EDItEUR ONIX; NISO JATS; IPTC NewsML-G2; Crossref membership obligations; BISG metadata practices; U.S. Copyright Office DMCA routing; and NIST configuration-management guidance with explicit refresh and decay posture.

Boundary logic prevents unsupported publication, metadata distribution, DOI registration, feed delivery, access mutation, system change, takedown action, confidentiality disclosure, and completion closure when required source evidence is missing or conflicting.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-blocking ambiguity is recorded as tenant, publication type, platform, jurisdiction, client, engagement, SOW, MSA, NDA, rights, accessibility, privacy, security, partner, approval, and source-record prerequisites.

Runtime remains specification-based; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, telemetry evidence, retrieval wiring, mutation safeguards, rollback controls, and non-regression validation.
